summary: Audit PRD V2 (docs/prd.md, just rewritten) vs prd_human_feedback A-M (intent fidelity for all 79 decisions) + the consistency watch-list + dropped-v1-content; synthesize findings + trivial fixes

findings.location: §7 (ends at line 6426, mid-M5); §7.0 lines 5953-5964; §7.2 header line 6077
findings.what: PRD V2 is TRUNCATED: the document ends inside §7 at M5's phase-task list (line 6426). §7.0 explicitly promises four parts — it enumerates '§7.3 Deployment — ... the About/Credits + license + content descriptors, the npm run verify release gate, and a brief "how to ship."' (line 5960-5962) and '§7.4 Risk register + scope-risk posture' (line 5963) — and §7.2 is titled 'Milestone roadmap (M0 … M7)' (line 6077). But M6 and M7 milestones, the entire §7.3 (Deployment), §7.3.1/§7.3.2, and §7.4 (Risk register) are absent. Dangling internal references confirm the cut: line 2391 'LICENSE, and itch content descriptors (§7.3.2).'; lines 40 and 5931 cite '§7.4.2'. This strands the milestone/deployment home of multiple Block L decisions assigned by the plan to M6/M7/§7.3/§7.4 (Q4 fun-gate at M6; Q56 perf risk in §7.4; Q50/Q51/Q52/Q53/Q54 deployment surface in §7.3; the budget-FLOOR M6 pacing gate). The design substance of Q50/Q51/Q52/Q54 survives in §2.21.1/§6.1.1, but the roadmap that §7 exists to provide is incomplete.
findings.question: §7 ends mid-M5 (line 6426); M6, M7, §7.3 Deployment, and §7.4 Risk register are missing despite being promised in §7.0 and cross-referenced (§7.3.2 at line 2391; §7.4.2 at lines 40/5931). Should the rewrite author the missing M6 (verifier flips + a11y acceptance + inline-SVG/audio + fun-proxy GATE), M7 (self-hosted fonts + LICENSE + About/Credits build + itch content descriptors + the deferred itch cross-iframe save smoke), §7.3 Deployment, and §7.4 Risk register (with the new fun / perf-memory / bounded-perks risk rows) to complete the doc?

findings.what: Q9 = 'macronize gōshi/rōnin.' The §6.6 romanization lint is self-contradictory and partially defeats Q9: line 5537 lists '*rōnin*' as a word the verifier MUST flag if un-macronized, yet lines 5538-5539 put '*ronin*' (un-macronized) in the Allow-list of 'naturalized English exonyms … exempt and may stay in their common form.' So the lint both requires and exempts rōnin. As a result un-macronized 'ronin' appears in prose at lines 1626, 2761, 2923, 5994, 6382, 6422 — directly against Q9's 'macronize rōnin project-wide.' (gōshi is consistently macronized — no 'goshi' hits, so that half of Q9 holds.)

findings.what: The Bestiary's reveal beat contradicts itself. Every §1/§2/§3 combat-reveal row places the Bestiary at R3 — e.g. §2.8.2 R3: '...the **bare auto-resolve loop** + **retreat** + the **Bestiary** (character (combat) level begins)'; §3.2 R3: 'the **Bestiary** panel (§2.9, fills by-encounter; the wild boar is the first grindable threat)'. But the §7.2.0 milestone map, which exists explicitly to 'pin which element lands in which milestone so the stagger has an unambiguous build home', says (line 6111-6112): 'The Bestiary / Equipment / Inventory panels themselves stay on the **M2b** half of the fixed seam' (M2b = R4), and M2b's lands list the Bestiary as 'the combat-reveal ladder's **R4 beat** — §2.8.2/§7.2.0' — citing §2.8.2 as authority for R4 even though §2.8.2 lists it at R3. This re-creates the very R3 multi-element UI-dump that FU4/FU12 declare retired ('the old R3 UI-dump').
findings.question: Which beat owns the Bestiary panel reveal — R3 (per the §1/§2/§3 reveal rows) or R4/M2b (per the §7.2.0 'unambiguous build home' table + M2a/M2b)? FU12 itself never lists the Bestiary, so this is a design call. Recommend adopting §7.2.0 as canon (Bestiary→R4) and striking '+ the Bestiary' from every R3 reveal cell, to honor FU4/FU12's no-R3-dump intent.
findings.proposed_old: | **R3** — combat rung | The **single starter weapon** + the **bare auto-resolve loop** + **retreat** + the **Bestiary** (character (combat) **level** begins). Combat stats start near-zero. |
findings.proposed_new: | **R3** — combat rung | The **single starter weapon** + the **bare auto-resolve loop** + **retreat** (character (combat) **level** begins). Combat stats start near-zero. *(The Bestiary panel lands at R4 with the loot→craft loop — §7.2.0.)* |

findings.what: The Equipment & Inventory panel reveal beat contradicts itself. §3.2 R3, §1.5.1 R3, §1.12 and §2.8(e) all bundle it at R3 — e.g. §3.2 R3: 'the single starter weapon (T0 starts with exactly one — Q15/FU13) + **Equipment & Inventory** panels (§2.10); the **Bestiary** panel'. But §3.5.1 and §4.6.9 R4 rows put it at R4 ('Graded weapon-durability bands surface with the simple Crafting loop + **Equipment/Inventory**'), and §7.2.0 + M2b explicitly place 'Equipment & Inventory (§2.10)' on the M2b/R4 half of the seam. The §3.2/§1.x 'starter weapon + Equipment & Inventory + Bestiary + auto-resolve + retreat' R3 bundle is precisely the retired R3 UI-dump FU4/FU12 say is gone.
findings.question: Does Equipment & Inventory reveal at R3 (alongside the starter weapon, so the weapon can be equipped) or at R4/M2b (per §3.5.1/§4.6.9/§7.2.0)? If R4 is canon, the R3 weapon must be auto-equipped with no panel; the §3.2/§1.5.1/§1.12/§2.8(e) R3 cells should drop 'Equipment & Inventory'. Recommend aligning to §7.2.0 (R4).

findings.location: docs/prd.md §3.5.1 roster table (line 2853) + §3.2 R6 (line 2636) — vs §7.2.0 (line 6104) + M2b goal/DoD (lines 6255, 6271)
findings.what: The reveal rung for the 2nd T0 weapon (the first of the '+2 across T0') contradicts itself. §3.5.1's roster-growth table puts it at R6: 'T0-R6 + a 2nd T0 beat | +2 more (completes Line 1 by end-T0)', and §3.2 R6 says 'the **2nd starter-line weapon** unlocks (the T0 roster grows +2 total across the tier)'. But §7.2.0 puts it at R4: 'R4 (loot→craft loop) | ... + a found/crafted **2nd T0 weapon** | M2b', and M2b's goal/DoD deliver 'a found/crafted 2nd T0 weapon unlocks' at R4. So the +2 T0 weapons are scheduled at R6+ in §3 but at R4 in §7 — the roster reveal beats are not byte-identical across §2.10/§3 reveal rows/§7 as the §6.5 'byte-identical' rule and FU13 require.
findings.question: At which rung does the first additional T0 weapon unlock — R4 (loot→craft loop, per §7.2.0/M2b) or R6 (per §3.2 R6 / §3.5.1 roster table)? The two +2-T0 weapons must be scheduled identically in §3 and §7. Recommend R4 + a later T0 beat to match the milestone build order (M2b closes the loot→craft loop).

findings.what: V2's §4 jumps straight from §4.6.9 (weapon roster) to §5, dropping three sections that v1 contained (archive/prd-v1.md: §4.7 'Producer / cost curves' L2843; §4.8 'PACING targets — the LOCKED time budget + the rank-by-rank pacing table' L2931; §4.9 'Levers index' L3063). These are the authored HOME and tie-out of human-signed A–K (Block G/H) acceptance criteria: G1 (≈28.5 h budget; T0 4.5 h / T1 8 h / T2 16 h) and G2 (≥30-min-per-rung floor with the R0 cold-open carve-out), plus §4.7.5 the E0→E3 build-cost row (Q8/D-033). In V2 they survive only as ~89 dangling references to non-existent sections. The omission breaks live tie-outs: §4.0 states it reads '~21K to match the §4.8.1 rung-by-rung sum exactly' (:3235) and that the koku bands 'come straight out of the §4.8 throughput model' (:3245) — against a table that does not exist; §4.1.1 back-solves the rung-meter from 'the same ≥30-min floor the §4.8 pacing model... use' (:1980); §5 says 'the E3 koku/Arms cost row is §4.7.5's job' (:4768); and the M5 Definition-of-Done gates on 'E1/E2 build fires on its §4.7.5 floor+cost' (:6323/:6325) and 'R0... exempt per §4.8.1' (:6015). No per-rung/rank-by-rank pacing table exists anywhere in V2 §4. Net effect: the locked pacing/budget acceptance criteria (G1/G2) and the §4.9 LOCKED list have no tied-out per-tier derivation — a regression from v1, and the document is not internally usable for M1+ balance until restored.
findings.question: Author the missing §4.7 (economy/cost curves, incl. the §4.7.5 E0→E3 build-cost row), §4.8 (per-tier rank-by-rank pacing tables re-derived for the sequential two-phase model — the ≥30-min-per-rung floor back-solve, the R0 exemption, the Phase-1 rung block + Phase-2 grind split, and the ≈28.5 h budget tie-out), and §4.9 (levers index / the LOCKED acceptance-criteria list) into V2 — porting and re-deriving v1 §4.7–§4.9 against the new model — to restore the home of the human-signed G1/G2 criteria and resolve the ~89 dangling §4.7/§4.8/§4.9 references and the broken §4.0/§4.1.1 reconciliations and M5 DoDs?

findings.what: The Name & Honour pillar is gated inconsistently at the v1 end-gate. §4.1's T2→T3 row (the v1 content-complete gate) lists Name as a REVEALED/GATED pillar: header '*Arms + Estate + Office + Name → 3–4; good in ALL …*' with a band triple 'Name | **28K** | **42K** | **60K**'. But §4.2.2 states the opposite: '**Name & Honour** gates no v1 tier, so its 0.30 is taken of its reference `TIER_REF_NAME` = … **15K (T1) / 140K (T2)**', and its seasonal JUDGE_K row reads 'Name & Honour | — (not gated) | **4,500** | **42,000**'. Two consequences: (a) direct contradiction — §4.1 puts Name in the 'good in ALL revealed pillars' T2 gate while §4.2.2 says Name gates no v1 tier; (b) the 70/30 accrual tie-out is broken for Name — the T2 seasonal stream ALONE (42,000) is ~1.5× the §4.1 Name good band (28K), and §4.2.2 normalizes Name against 140K, not 28K, so the §6.6.1 'accrual tie-out' invariant cannot hold for Name. §4.2.1's T2 tie-out (line 3520) also omits Name entirely. The '3–4' wobble in §1.6.3/§2.16 is the same unresolved question surfacing.
findings.question: Is Name & Honour a GATED pillar at the v1 T2→T3 end-gate (revealed set T2 = 4, good-band 28K, must be 'good') or NOT gated in v1 (revealed set T2 = 3 = Arms+Estate+Office, Name merely 'surfacing' cosmetically)? §4.1 assumes the former; §4.2.1/§4.2.2 assume the latter. Pick one and reconcile: either drop Name from the §4.1 T2→T3 'good in ALL' profile and its 28K band, or rework §4.2.2 so Name's seasonal/TIER_REF tie out to a real 28K good band (currently 42K seasonal > 28K good).

findings.category: deed inventory / revealed-pillar schedule / per-event cap
findings.location: §4.2.1 (line 3495; contrast Office line 3493; tie-out lines 3504–3525)
findings.what: The §4.2.1 deed table gives Name PREMATURE per-tier base values and caps that match no §4.1 band, contradicting the revealed-pillar schedule (Name reveals at T2). The row 'Lord's recognition / off foreclosure list | Name | 20 | 160 | 1.6K | T0 40 · T1 400 · T2 3.2K' assigns Name a T0 base of **20** and a T0 per-event cap of **40** — implying a T0 Name good-band of 1K (0.04·1K=40) that does not exist (T0 is the 2-pillar special: Arms+Estate only). By contrast the Office row correctly shows '—' for T0 because Office is unrevealed at T0 — yet Name reveals even LATER (T2), so it should be at least as dashed. The Name cap column (T0 40 / T1 400 / T2 3.2K) implies good-bands 1K / 10K / 80K, which match NEITHER §4.1 (Name good = 28K at T2 → cap should be 0.04·28K = 1.12K, not 3.2K) NOR §4.2.2's TIER_REF_NAME (15K/140K). Name is never itemized in any §4.2.1 70%-tie-out (T0, T1, or T2 lines 3504–3525 all omit it), so these base values are un-tied-out leftovers.
findings.question: Once watchlist-1 resolves Name's gating, fix the §4.2.1 Name deed row to match: if Name is unrevealed before T2, set its T0 (and T1) base + cap to '—' (as Office's T0 is dashed); and recompute the surviving Name caps as 0.04·(Name good band) so T2 cap = 0.04·28K ≈ 1.12K rather than the current 3.2K.
findings.proposed_old: | Lord's recognition / off foreclosure list | Name | 20 | 160 | 1.6K | T0 40 · T1 400 · T2 3.2K |

findings.location: §3.7.2 T4 ladder (header line 3069; rows lines 3090–3091) vs estate stages (§2.17 line 2133, §7.1.1 line 6000, §5 line 4907)
findings.what: The label 'E#' is overloaded across two unrelated ladders, and the V2 E3-unpark sharpens the collision. Estate STAGES are E0→E5 (E0→E3 authored in v1, E4–E5 parked — §2.17/§7.1.1). findings.question: Rename one of the two E-ladders to remove the overload — e.g. relabel the T4 rung ladder away from E0→E7 (the rungs collide with the E0→E5 estate-stage names), or namespace it (e.g. 'T4-E0…E7' / a distinct letter). Which ladder should be renamed?

findings.location: §4.2.1 deed table (line 3495) + cap text (lines 3468–3473) + T2 tie-out (lines 3520–3525), vs §4.1 bands (lines 3343–3346) and the revealed-set (§1.6.4 line 618 / §2.16(e) / §3.0.1 line 2565)

(b) The cap column implies Name good-bands of 1K/10K/80K (cap=0.04·good), none of which exist in §4.1, where Name first appears at T2 with good=28K → correct cap = 0.04·28K = 1.12K, NOT the listed 3.2K. (d) The T2 accrual tie-out (lines 3520–3525) itemizes Estate/Arms/Office but OMITS Name entirely, so the 'deeds = 70% of the good band' proof — which §6.6.1's accrual-tie-out verifier asserts — is missing for a pillar the T2→T3 gate REQUIRES good-in (28K → 70% = 19,600 ip).
findings.question: §4.2.1's Name deed row (T0 base 20 / T1 base 160 and caps T0 40 · T1 400 · T2 3.2K) doesn't match the V2 revealed-set (Name reveals only at T2) or the V2 §4.1 Name band (T2 good=28K, so cap should be ~1.12K not 3.2K, and base 1.6K exceeds it), and the T2 tie-out omits Name's 19,600 ip. findings.what: Sections disagree on WHEN the Combat Rank rung-meter begins being fed. §4.1.1 (the authoritative rung-meter law) lists 'R3 Yard-hand | Combat Rank | survive the first fight · drill reps · first pest skirmishes' — i.e. R3 is a Combat-Rank-gated rung, fed from R3 — and §7 M2a (R3) line 6245 confirms 'curated combat activities seed the Combat Rank rung-meter' at R3 (line 6221: 'per-rung curated combat activities → the Combat Rank rung-meter'). synthesis.verdict: NOT READY — blocking structural defects. PRD V2 is materially truncated in two places and cannot pass the human glance or the §7 code-unlock gate as-is. (1) §4 ends at §4.6.9 (line 4170) and jumps to §5: the entire balance spine — §4.7 (producer/cost/yield curves incl. the §4.7.5 E0→E3 build-cost row), §4.8 (the LOCKED rank-by-rank pacing tables, incl. the §4.8.1 koku column the rest of §4/§6/§7 tie out against and the budget-FLOOR model), and §4.9 (levers/LOCKED-constants index) — is GONE, leaving ~89 dangling cross-refs and the human-signed G1/G2 pacing/budget acceptance criteria with no tied-out home. (2) §7 ends mid-§7.2 at M5 (line 6426): M6 (verifier flips + a11y + inline-SVG/audio + the fun-proxy GATE + balance pass), M7 (self-hosted fonts + LICENSE + About/Credits + itch content descriptors + the deferred cross-iframe save smoke), §7.2.1, §7.3 Deployment (+ §7.3.1/§7.3.2), §7.4 Risk register (+ new fun/perf rows), and §7.5 are absent despite being promised by §7.0 and the §7.2 'M0…M7' heading. The design SUBSTANCE of most Block L+M decisions survives elsewhere, but the truncation strands Q4 (fun-gate), Q53 (content descriptors), Q56 (perf deferral), and the G1/G2 LOCKED criteria. Beyond the truncation, the rewrite surfaced one genuinely unresolved design issue (Name & Honour gating + its broken accrual tie-out) and a set of reveal-ladder seam contradictions (§3/§2 vs the §7.2.0 milestone map) that need a human call. Five clerical fixes are apply-ready. Recommendation: re-author the two dropped tails from v1 against the V2 model, resolve the Name-gating + reveal-seam questions, then re-audit.

clusters.theme: Name & Honour pillar: gating contradiction + broken 70/30 accrual tie-out
clusters.summary: The only substantive (non-truncation) design defect the rewrite surfaced. §4.1's T2→T3 end-gate lists Name as a REVEALED/GATED pillar ('good in ALL', band 28K/42K/60K), but §4.2.2 says 'Name & Honour gates no v1 tier' and normalizes it against TIER_REF_NAME 15K/140K with a T2 seasonal stream of 42,000 — which ALONE is ~1.5× the §4.1 28K good band, so the §6.6.1 accrual-tie-out invariant cannot hold for Name. §4.2.1's deed row gives Name premature T0/T1 base values + caps (T0 40·T1 400·T2 3.2K) that match no §4.1 band and contradict the revealed-set (Name reveals only at T2; Office is correctly dashed at T0 but Name — revealing later — is not); the T2 base 1.6K exceeds its own correct cap (0.04·28K≈1.12K), violating §4.2.1's stated rule; and the T2 70%-tie-out omits Name entirely (no 19,600 ip line). Resolution must pick Name's gating first, then re-derive the deed row + tie-out.

human_questions.q: §4.7 (yields/crafting-cost/loot-tables/auto-producers + the §4.7.5 E0→E3 build-cost row), §4.8 (the LOCKED rank-by-rank pacing tables + budget-FLOOR model + the planned NEW §4.8.1b Phase-2 grind block), and §4.9 (levers/LOCKED-constants index) are ENTIRELY ABSENT despite ~89 inbound refs and the change plan requiring them kept+extended. Not internally usable for M1+ balance until restored.

human_questions.q: §7 ends mid-M5 (line 6426). Author the missing M6 (verifier flips + a11y acceptance + inline-SVG/audio + the fun-proxy GATE + balance-pass-to-floor + T3 Daikan stub), M7 (self-hosted OFL fonts + LICENSE + About/Credits build + itch content descriptors + the deferred cross-iframe save smoke), §7.2.1, §7.3 Deployment (+ §7.3.1 Assets / §7.3.2 About-Credits-licensing), §7.4 Risk register (with new R6-fun / R7-perf rows), and §7.5 — per the change plan's 85 §7 changes?

human_questions.q: Is Name & Honour a GATED pillar at the v1 T2→T3 end-gate (revealed set T2=4, good-band 28K, must be 'good' — per §4.1) or NOT gated in v1 (T2=3 = Arms+Estate+Office, Name merely surfacing — per §4.2.1/§4.2.2)? Pick one, then reconcile: either drop Name from §4.1's 'good in ALL' profile + its 28K band, or rework §4.2.2 so Name's seasonal (currently 42K > the 28K good band) and TIER_REF tie out to a real good band; and re-derive the §4.2.1 Name deed row (remove orphan T0/T1 bases, recompute caps as 0.04·good, add the missing T2 70%-tie-out line).
human_questions.why: Genuine unresolved design contradiction surfaced by the rewrite — §4.1 and §4.2.1/§4.2.2 assume opposite answers, the 70/30 accrual tie-out (a §6.6.1 machine-checked invariant) is broken for Name, and a deed base (1.6K) exceeds its own cap. The '3–4 revealed pillars' wobble in §1.6.3/§2.16 is the same question.

human_questions.q: Resolve the four reveal-ladder seam contradictions between the §1/§2/§3 reveal rows and the §7.2.0 'unambiguous build home' map: (1) Bestiary panel R3 or R4? (2) Equipment & Inventory R3 or R4? (3) the 2nd T0 weapon R4 or R6? (4) the Combat Rank rung-meter feed R3 or R5? Recommend adopting §7.2.0 + the §4.1.1 rung-law as canon (Bestiary→R4, Eqp&Inv→R4 with the R3 weapon auto-equipped, 2nd T0 weapon→R4, meter-feed→R3) and sweeping the stale cells, to honor FU4/FU12's no-R3-UI-dump intent and keep an R3 Combat-Rank rung promotable.
human_questions.why: Multiple §1/§2/§3 cells re-create the retired R3 UI-dump; the R5-vs-R3 meter-feed disagreement is a live mechanical contradiction (an R3 Combat-Rank-gated rung can't promote if its meter only feeds from R5). The §6.5 byte-identical roster-schedule rule is also violated by the 2nd-T0-weapon R4/R6 split.

human_questions.q: In §4.2.2, does seasonal pillar credit accrue per-season from season 1 (as the worked 8-season tie-out shows, largest delta at s1) or Phase-2-only with a large first jump (as the FU7 prose and the §6.6/§6.6.1 'no deed value before the tier's final rung' verifier require)? Clarify which is canonical and relabel the table as a non-posting basis-ramp illustration or recompute it as the actual Phase-2 posting schedule.
human_questions.why: Table and prose directly disagree on a load-bearing FU7 invariant that is machine-checked; leaves the tie-out ambiguous.

human_questions.q: After removing 'ronin' from the §6.6 macron allow-list (trivial_fix fidelity-L-5), sweep the residual un-macronized 'ronin' → 'rōnin' in prose at lines 1626/2761/2923/5994/6382/6422 so the now-stricter lint passes. Confirm rōnin should be macronized in ALL prose (Q9) with no exceptions.
human_questions.why: Applying only the allow-list edit without the prose sweep leaves the doc self-inconsistent (lint requires the macron but 6 prose lines lack it). Needs confirmation that none of these are intentional exonym uses.

human_questions.q: §5 T2.6 calls the rōnin drops 'the first decent FOUND blades (a worn kodachi, a fitted yari)', but kodachi/yari are T0/T1 weapons (§4.6.9) already found at T1 (§5 T1.6), and T2 opens the staff line. Reword to reference T2/staff-line found gear and drop 'first decent'?
human_questions.why: Contradicts the authoritative §4.6.9 roster and duplicates the T1.6 found-gear beat.

human_questions.q: The 'E#' label is overloaded: estate STAGES E0→E5 vs the §3.7.2 T4 RUNG ladder E0→E7 — and the E3-unpark makes 'E3' mean both the authored estate stage 'Prosperous' and T4 rung 3. Rename/namespace one ladder (e.g. 'T4-E0…E7')?
human_questions.why: Reader-confusion risk between an authored v1 estate stage and a roadmap Edo rung; low but worth a quick disambiguation.

human_questions.q: The §4.6.9 weapon roster (and §2.10.1/§6.5/items.ts) omits a per-weapon damage/weaponBase column that the §4.6.1 attackPower formula requires (only the yari's ≈3 is given in §4.6.6 prose). Add a weaponBase column to the roster, or was it intended to live in the dropped §4.7? Resolve as part of the §4.7 re-authoring.
human_questions.why: No per-weapon damage value exists anywhere for any weapon but the starter — the combat math is unspecified for the roster.

synthesis.fidelity_summary: Across the 79 Block L+M decisions and the still-binding A–K set, V2 preserves most DESIGN SUBSTANCE in §1–§6 but the two truncations strand a meaningful subset and leave the doc not internally usable for M1+ balance. HONOURED: the hybrid-gate model, two-phase Phase-1/Phase-2 accrual, rung-meter law (§4.1.1), per-weapon baseSpeed, graded durability bands, FU13 single-starter-weapon, the Q11 weir-jizō rename (in §5), the Q39 fictionalised-name renames (Obaa Kuni etc., except one §7.1.3 leftover), Origin track zero-mechanical-gift + G6-truth/O5-missable split, FU20 broadened combos (in §4.3.1, but not swept into §6.6.1), and the §2.21.1/§6.1.1 deployment substance of Q50/Q51/Q52/Q54. PARTIALLY/NOT HONOURED due to dropped sections: G1/G2 LOCKED pacing+budget criteria have NO tied-out home (§4.8 gone); Q8/D-033 E0→E3 cost row gone (§4.7.5 absent); Q4 fun-proxies only at M1 (M3 DoD omits them, the M6 fun-GATE milestone + the required fun risk row are absent); Q56 perf/memory deferral note ENTIRELY unreflected (§7.4 home gone); Q53 itch content descriptors never enumerated (only dangling §7.3.2 pointers); the M6/M7 milestone assignments, §7.3 Deployment, and §7.4 Risk register simply do not exist. CONTRADICTED: Q9 (macronize rōnin) is defeated by the §6.6 allow-list still exempting 'ronin' (+ 6 un-macronized prose hits). INTERNALLY INCONSISTENT (rewrite-surfaced): Name & Honour gating + accrual tie-out (§4.1 vs §4.2.x), FU4/FU12 reveal-ladder seam (§1/§2/§3 vs §7.2.0 on Bestiary/Equipment/2nd-T0-weapon/meter-feed beats), and the FU7 no-Phase-1-accrual invariant (§4.2.2 prose vs worked table). Net: the rewrite's prose/design layer is largely faithful, but ~114 dangling cross-references to the dropped §4.7/§4.8/§4.9 and §7-tail sections, plus the orphaned LOCKED criteria, mean V2 does NOT yet fully honour the binding decision set and must be completed before sign-off.
